title: Prisma: typed access to Postgres
objective: See how schema.prisma becomes real TypeScript types, and why we share one client.

body.p: schema.prisma describes your tables. Running 'npx prisma generate' writes a fully-typed client — queries are type-checked against your actual columns.
Every route file used to run 'new PrismaClient()' on its own, each opening its own connection pool. Now every file imports one shared instance from prisma.ts.

code.text:
import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';

export const prisma = new PrismaClient();

quiz.q: Why share one PrismaClient instance across the app?
options: Each instance opens its own database connection pool; many instances waste connections
PrismaClient can only be created once per process by law
